content: Added a runtime, in-app, team-managed way for the desktop (and web/mobile) app to point at a team's OWN self-hosted server with no rebuild. New server-config.js (setServer/useNaridon/normalizeServerUrl/currentServerHost/openServerDialog) writes a single localStorage `notionless_server_url`; config.js SIGNALING_URL and CLOUD_SYNC_URL getters now derive `/signaling` and `/yjs` from that base via teamServerBase(), defaulting to Naridon's oss.naridon.com until set. Wired entry points: a server gear in the Teams sidebar header (main.js) and a Team-server row in the cloud-sync popover (cloud-sync.js), both opening openServerDialog(). Once connected nothing depends on Naridon; the always-on checkbox reuses the existing notionless_cloud_sync_disabled opt-out p2p.js honors. Updated docs/SELF_HOSTING.md desktop-connect section.

Refined P2PNetwork constructor so an EXPLICIT team-server signaling URL (set via server-config.js) is honored even in localhost dev — previously isLocalDev unconditionally dropped the relay, which would hide the in-app "connect to your team's server" override under `pnpm run dev`. The official oss.naridon.com default stays dev-skipped, so dev still doesn't hit the public relay unless a team opts in.

Fixed editor page scroll so the whole page scrolls as one (Notion-style): converted .editor-container into the scroll viewport (overflow-y:auto, display:block) and let CodeMirror grow to content (#editor/.cm-editor height:auto, .cm-scroller overflow:visible) instead of trapping scroll inside .cm-scroller. Now the page cover/icon/title (.page-header) and the Linked references panel (.backlinks-panel) scroll together with the editor body. Added a 45vh min-height on .cm-content so short notes keep a click target.

Fixed comment-popover markdown: added _mdInline() in comments.js (HTML-escape then inline-markdown: bold/italic/code/strike/highlight/safe-links) and routed _fmt() + the three quoted/snippet renders (showNewComment, _renderPopoverContent, _renderPanel) through it so document markdown renders instead of showing raw ** markers. Fixed editor layout regression + uncaught Yjs 'clock' crash: hardened DocumentEngine.destroy() to wrap every provider/awareness/doc teardown step in try/catch (a y-protocols awareness 'clock' throw was escaping, aborting teardown and breaking CodeMirror's shared base-theme StyleModule refcount so the next editor mounted without flex-grow and collapsed to per-character wrapping); restored .editor-container/#editor to flex-column with flex-shrink:0 children + width:100% on cm-editor/cm-scroller as a width safety-net while keeping whole-page scroll.

Gave the team-server connect flow a real post-connect experience in server-config.js. Added pingServer() (GET /health with AbortController timeout) and rewrote openServerDialog's connect handler into a validating flow: Connecting… → reachable shows green "Connected ✓" then commits, unreachable falls back to an amber "Connect anyway" (covers CORS-blocked/older boxes) with re-validation on input edit. Added showServerToast()/flashConnectToastIfPending() one-shot post-reload confirmation toast (host + always-on state), keyed off a CONNECTED_FLASH_KEY localStorage flag set by commit() and the Naridon reset; wired flashConnectToastIfPending() into main.js init.

Upgraded the Company Brain composer model picker. In rag-engine.js added modelService/modelEffortLevels/modelTier helpers, a modelEffort field (persisted as `effort`), enriched listModels() so each entry carries {service,desc,efforts} plus per-backend currentEffort/effortApplies, setModelEffort(), and _apiExtra() which injects reasoning_effort into all three OpenAI-compatible request bodies (_generateApi, _completeOnce, _streamCompletion). In brain-drawer.js openModelMenu() now renders a real brand SVG (serviceLogo) + tier hint + reasoning badge per model and a Reasoning-effort segment for capable models; updateModelPill() shows the model's brand logo and effort suffix; added _modelHasEffort/_modelLogoKind. Models stay live-fetched (Ollama /api/tags, provider /models). style.css styles the logos, tiers, and effort pills.

Made the Brain model picker fetch REAL model info from the agent and wire Claude effort. In src/main/index.js the ai:claude-code handler now runs --output-format json (parsing .result for the answer, capturing the exact model id from modelUsage, plus token usage, cost, contextWindow, maxOutputTokens) and passes --effort. In rag-engine.js added CLAUDE_EFFORTS, claudeResolved/lastUsage state, _claudeEffort() and _recordClaudeResult() (persists alias→exact-id map), passed effort + captured results in both claude-code invocations, and extended listModels() so claude-code shows the exact resolved id as the tier and exposes session effort levels (effortApplies now covers claude-code). In brain-drawer.js updateModelPill() shows the exact id + valid effort suffix + a usage tooltip, _modelHasEffort() covers claude-code, and renderUsageFooter() prints a per-answer token/cost line. style.css styles the usage footer.

Brain UX + capability pass: (1) Replaced every fa-brain icon with the monochrome sparkIcon glyph (brain-drawer openModelMenu reason/effort-head, main.js sidebar Company Brain item) and removed the pink #7c6df2 accents in style.css for neutral muted tones, adding .brain-spark sizing classes — per user's "hate the brain icon, never use pink, use a spark". (2) Added warmClaude()/claudeWarm cache in src/main/index.js called at app.whenReady so a free `claude --version` probe primes the login-PATH resolver + node + binary cache at app open (no Brain cold start); ai:claude-code-available now serves the warm cache. (3) Added the recent_changes builtin tool + _toolRecentChanges + _humanAgo to rag-engine.js so the Company Brain can answer "what changed recently?" by ranking indexed notes on fs mtime.

Release 1.1.0 prep: bumped package.json version 1.0.8→1.1.0 and added a universal `zip` target to build.mac.target so electron-updater has a usable macOS auto-update artifact (dmg alone can't be applied by Squirrel.Mac); updated tests/brain-tools.test.mjs to expect the six built-ins now that recent_changes is registered; gitignored local AI-tooling cruft.

Fix universal macOS build: exclude .aura/** (and dist/dist-web/.gemini/producthunt) from build.files. Aura was writing snapshot JSON into .aura/ between the x64 and arm64 packaging passes, so @electron/universal mergeASARs saw arch-divergent files and aborted. Excluding dev-tooling dirs from the app bundle makes the merge deterministic.
